exampleSentences: Anh muốn đi ra ngoài ăn tối với em tối nay không?
Em thường ăn sáng lúc bảy giờ trước khi đi làm.
Chị ấy không thích ăn cay nên luôn chọn món nhẹ.
notes:
ăn = to eat, to consume food. It’s the basic verb you’ll use for meals and snacks.

Use ăn + food (ăn cơm, ăn bánh, ăn phở). You can also say ăn + object more generally for consuming things like medicine (ăn thuốc).

It combines a lot with other words: ăn sáng / ăn trưa / ăn tối for meals, and ăn với + something to say what you eat something with.

Very common extended meanings: ăn can mean “to win” (ăn điểm) or “to match/go well with” (like colors that look good together), so don’t always translate it as “eat.”

translation.en: I want to eat this dish.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Do you want to go out to eat dinner with me tonight?
I usually eat breakfast at seven o'clock before going to work.
She does not like to eat spicy food so she always chooses light dishes.

translation.en: Do you want to eat rice?

It combines a lot with other words: **ăn sáng / ăn trưa / ăn tối** for meals, and **ăn với + something** to say what you eat something with.

Very common extended meanings: **ăn** can mean “to win” (ăn điểm) or “to match/go well with” (like colors that look good together), so don’t always translate it as “eat.”
